title: sida senegalensis
intro: Sida senegalensis, also known as African milk tree or devil's cotton, is a plant species in the mallow family Malvaceae. It has been traditionally used in various parts of the world for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Sida senegalensis include the treatment of fever, respiratory problems, and digestive issues, as well as its potential role in reducing inflammation and improving wound healing. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it can also be used to make herbal teas and tinctures.
